Obtain internal reviews. 4. Finalize and submit. Deliverables: Complete DFG grant proposal.

• Grant Writer

Prepare preliminary applications. Deliverables: List of potential alternative funding sources.

• Research Technicians

Objective: Evaluate the suitability and feasibility of each identified recruitment channel.
Scope: This includes assessing the target audience, cost-effectiveness, and potential ethical considerations.
Steps: Conduct market research, analyze historical data, and consult with experts.
Deliverables: A report summarizing the suitability and feasibility of each channel, including recommendations for implementation.

• Study Coordinator
• Principal Investigator
• Clinical Psychologist

Objective: Formalize agreements with selected recruitment channels.

• Study Coordinator
• Principal Investigator
• Legal Counsel

Develop Recruitment Materials

Description:
Objective: Create compelling recruitment materials tailored to each channel.
Scope: This includes designing advertisements, writing brochures, and developing online content.
Steps: Develop materials, obtain feedback, and revise as needed.
Deliverables: A set of recruitment materials for each selected channel.

Resources needed:
• Study Coordinator
• Research Technicians
• Graphic Designer

Review Literature on Stringency Criteria

Description:
Objective: Conduct a comprehensive review of existing literature to identify relevant and validated stringency criteria for NREM parasomnia research.
Scope: This includes searching databases (PubMed, Scopus, Web of Science) for studies on parasomnia, sleep disorders, and related neurological conditions. The review will focus on inclusion/exclusion criteria used in previous studies, their rationale, and their impact on sample characteristics and event capture.
Steps:
1. Define search terms and keywords.
2. Conduct literature search.
3. Screen articles for relevance.
4. Extract data on stringency criteria.
5. Summarize findings and identify potential criteria for the current study.
Deliverables: A summary report of the literature review, including a list of potential stringency criteria and their rationale.

Resources needed:
• Principal Investigator
• Postdoctoral Researchers
• Literature databases
• Reference management software

Consult Sleep Disorder Neurologist

Description:
Objective: Obtain expert opinion from a neurologist specializing in sleep disorders to validate the proposed stringency criteria and ensure their clinical relevance.
Scope: This involves scheduling a consultation with a neurologist, presenting the proposed criteria, and discussing their potential impact on participant selection and data interpretation.
Steps:
1. Identify a suitable neurologist.
2. Schedule a consultation.
3. Prepare a presentation of the proposed criteria.
4. Conduct the consultation.
5. Document the neurologist's feedback and recommendations.
Deliverables: A written summary of the neurologist's feedback and recommendations, including any suggested modifications to the stringency criteria.

Resources needed:
• Principal Investigator
• Neurologist
• Presentation materials

Pilot Test Stringency Criteria

Description:
Objective: Conduct a pilot test of the proposed stringency criteria to assess their feasibility and impact on participant recruitment and event capture.
Scope: This involves applying the criteria to a small sample of potential participants and evaluating the number of eligible participants, the characteristics of the recruited sample, and the number of parasomnia events captured.
Steps:
1. Recruit a small sample of potential participants.
2. Apply the stringency criteria.
3. Assess the number of eligible participants.
4. Collect data on participant characteristics and event capture.
5. Analyze the data and evaluate the impact of the criteria.
Deliverables: A report summarizing the results of the pilot test, including the number of eligible participants, the characteristics of the recruited sample, and the number of parasomnia events captured.

Resources needed:
• Study Coordinator
• Research Technicians
• Potential participants
• Data collection equipment

project_name: Refine Criteria Based on Pilot Data
project_description:
Refine Criteria Based on Pilot Data

Description:
Objective: Refine the stringency criteria based on the results of the pilot test and the neurologist's feedback.
Scope: This involves analyzing the pilot test data, considering the neurologist's recommendations, and making any necessary adjustments to the criteria to optimize participant recruitment and event capture.
Steps:
1. Analyze the pilot test data.
2. Review the neurologist's feedback.
3. Identify potential modifications to the criteria.
4. Discuss the proposed modifications with the research team.
5. Finalize the refined stringency criteria.
Deliverables: A final version of the stringency criteria, incorporating the results of the pilot test and the neurologist's feedback.

Resources needed:
• Principal Investigator
• Postdoctoral Researchers
• Pilot test data
• Neurologist's feedback

project_name: Define EEG montage and sensor placement
project_description:
Define EEG montage and sensor placement

Description:
Determine the optimal EEG montage and sensor placement strategy to maximize signal quality and minimize artifacts. This includes selecting appropriate electrode locations, considering head size and shape variations, and establishing standardized procedures for sensor application.

Define the parameters for polysomnography (PSG) data acquisition, including sampling rates, filter settings, and impedance thresholds. This involves optimizing the settings for each channel (EEG, EOG, EMG, ECG, respiratory sensors, etc.) to ensure accurate and reliable data collection.

Resources needed:
• Sleep Technologist
• PSG equipment
• PSG software

Establish procedures for monitoring and ensuring the quality of acquired data. This includes implementing real-time artifact detection, developing protocols for addressing data quality issues, and establishing criteria for data rejection or re-acquisition.

• Sleep Technologist
• PSG equipment
• EEG equipment
• Data analysis software

Conduct pilot studies to evaluate the effectiveness and feasibility of the data acquisition protocols. This involves collecting data from a small number of participants, analyzing the data quality, and refining the protocols based on the results.

Scope: This includes analyzing the time required for each data collection session, data annotation, and participant management.
Steps: 1. Estimate the time required for each data collection activity. 2. Project the number of participants enrolled per month. 3. Calculate the total workload in hours per month. 4. Determine the number of staff required to meet the workload demands.
